channel.title: Eugenics: A Critical History & Bioethics Archive
channel.description: A critical education and archive project documenting the history, harms, and modern bioethics legacy of eugenics and scientific racism.

item.title: Eugenics and Disability Rights
item.description: Why disability history and disability rights are central to understanding and critiquing eugenics.

item.title: Eugenics and Immigration Policy
item.description: How eugenic ideas shaped immigration restriction, national identity, and racialized ideas about belonging.

item.title: Eugenics in Nazi Germany
item.description: How Nazi racial hygiene used eugenic ideology to justify compulsory sterilization, persecution, and the murder of disabled people.

item.title: How Eugenics Entered Schools and Public Health
item.description: How eugenic ideas circulated through classrooms, public-health campaigns, fairs, textbooks, and social reform language.

item.title: Eugenics, IQ, and the Misuse of Heredity
item.description: How eugenicists misused intelligence testing, heredity, and statistics to rank people and justify harmful policy.

item.title: What Was the Eugenics Record Office?
item.description: A critical overview of the Eugenics Record Office as an institution that collected family data and promoted eugenic policy.

item.title: A Timeline of Eugenics, 1883-Present
item.description: A critical timeline of eugenics from the coining of the term to modern debates about genetics and bioethics.

item.title: Is There Such a Thing as Modern Eugenics?
item.description: A careful guide to the modern eugenics debate around genetic testing, embryo screening, enhancement, disability rights, and social pressure.

item.title: Teaching Eugenics Responsibly
item.description: A guide for educators teaching eugenics history without normalizing pseudoscience, reproducing harm, or centering propaganda.

item.title: Why Eugenics Is Scientifically Wrong
item.description: Why eugenics misread heredity, oversimplified complex traits, and turned prejudice into policy.

item.title: Buck v. Bell and Forced Sterilization
item.description: Why Buck v. Bell remains central to the legal and ethical history of eugenic sterilization.

item.title: Charles Davenport and Institutional Eugenics
item.description: A critical profile of Charles Davenport, the Eugenics Record Office, and institutional data power in U.S. eugenics.

item.title: CRISPR, Enhancement, and New Eugenics Claims
item.description: How genome editing debates can distinguish therapy, enhancement, governance, rights, and eugenics risks.

item.title: Eugenics and Race
item.description: How eugenic movements used race as a false biological ranking and how modern genetics rejects racial hierarchy.

item.title: Eugenics and Scientific Racism
item.description: How eugenics and scientific racism reinforced one another through measurement, hierarchy, public policy, and institutional authority.

item.title: Eugenics in Britain
item.description: A critical overview of British eugenics, Francis Galton, UCL legacies, class, empire, and institutional reckoning.

item.title: Eugenics in Canada
item.description: A critical overview of Canadian eugenics, with attention to Alberta, British Columbia, institutional power, and archive accountability.

item.title: Eugenics in Sweden
item.description: A critical overview of Swedish sterilization history and what it shows about welfare states, medical authority, and coercive policy.

item.title: Eugenics in the United States
item.description: A critical overview of U.S. eugenics, including sterilization laws, immigration policy, public health, education, and institutional authority.

item.title: Eugenics vs. Genetics
item.description: How to distinguish genetics as a scientific field from eugenics as an ideology and policy movement.

item.title: Forced Sterilization Laws
item.description: How forced sterilization laws worked, whom they targeted, and why they remain central to the history of eugenics.

item.title: Francis Galton and the Origin of Eugenics
item.description: A critical profile of Francis Galton, the coining of eugenics, and the imperial and statistical context around the term.

item.title: Genetic Testing, Embryo Selection, and Ethical Boundaries
item.description: How to discuss genetic testing and embryo selection without collapsing voluntary care into eugenics or ignoring eugenic risks.

item.title: Is Eugenics Pseudoscience?
item.description: Why eugenics borrowed scientific language while failing scientific, ethical, and human-rights standards.

item.title: What Is Eugenics?
item.description: A clear definition of eugenics, why it was harmful, and how to study the term without endorsing its claims.
